Look up Chinese surnames, compare common family names, and understand meaning, origin, pinyin, and romanization notes in one place.

Hundred Family Surnames

Trace Chinese family names through characters, origins, and heritage.

Explore common Chinese surnames with character notes, pinyin, romanized variants, Baijiaxing context, and practical English explanations.

Pinyinvariant spelling support

Top Surnames

Start with major Chinese family names

李 #1 Li Lee, Lei

Li is one of the most common Chinese surnames and appears across many regional romanization systems.

 王 #2 Wang Wong, Ong

Wang is widely used in Mandarin and appears as Wong or Ong in some Cantonese, Hokkien, or regional communities.

 张 #3 Zhang Cheung, Chang

Zhang is a major Mandarin surname with common romanized forms such as Cheung and Chang in overseas communities.

 刘 #4 Liu Lau, Low

Liu is historically important and strongly represented in Chinese history and overseas Chinese communities.

 陈 #5 Chen Chan, Tan

Chen is a very common Chinese surname, while Chan and Tan are common regional romanizations.

 杨 #6 Yang Yeung

Yang is a common surname across Mandarin-speaking regions and appears as Yeung in some Cantonese romanization.

 黄 #7 Huang Wong, Ng

Huang is common in Mandarin contexts; Wong and Ng can appear as related regional romanization forms depending on language and family history.

 赵 #8 Zhao Chao, Chiu

Zhao is famous as the first surname in the traditional Hundred Family Surnames text.

 吴 #9 Wu Ng, Woo

Wu is common in Mandarin, while Ng and Woo are frequent overseas romanization forms.

 周 #10 Zhou Chou, Chow

Zhou appears in Mandarin as Zhou and in older or regional romanizations as Chou or Chow.

Research Paths

Read a surname from four useful angles

01Meaning

Read the character meaning without overclaiming genealogy.

 02Origin

Connect surnames with states, places, titles, and lineage context.

 03Common Names

Compare high-frequency surnames with pinyin and variants.

 04Baijiaxing

Use the classic text as a cultural reference, not a census list.

Chinese Surname Guide

This site explains Chinese surnames, common romanizations, meanings, and origins for educational reference. It is not a genealogy verification service.
